verdict_reasoning: WT1 is a nuclear transcription factor with zinc-finger and DNA-binding domains that localize it to the nucleus for transcriptional regulation. It has no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, lipidation signals, or documented membrane trafficking pathway. While WT1-derived peptides are presented on MHC (basis for some immunotherapy programs), the protein body itself is strictly nuclear and not surface-accessible; pMHC presentation alone does not establish surface accessibility for the protein.
